summary: The Sprint 8 changes are performance-neutral to slightly positive. The analytics module split maintains the existing Redis caching strategy with 1-hour TTL. The frontend split into section components means each section fetches independently with staleTime: 3_600_000 — this is correct. The main performance concern is get_metrics in analytics_metrics.py which issues N sequential Redis cache reads (one per ticker) in the exception fallback path. The fx-gain-loss endpoint also issues N sequential get_cached_price calls in a for-loop when Redis does not have the data. Both should use asyncio.gather. No N+1 DB queries found — all queries use IN clauses.

findings.title: Sequential get_cached_price calls in fx-gain-loss endpoint
findings.detail: The for loop over overseas holdings calls `await _get_cached_price(h.ticker)` sequentially for each holding. With 10 overseas holdings, this is 10 sequential Redis round-trips. This adds latency proportional to the number of holdings.
findings.fix: Use asyncio.gather: price_tasks = [_get_cached_price(h.ticker) for h in overseas]; prices = await asyncio.gather(*price_tasks). Then zip prices with overseas holdings.

findings.title: Sequential cache reads in get_metrics exception fallback
findings.detail: In the except block after asyncio.gather fails, individual _get_cached_price calls are made per ticker in a loop. While the main gather path is already parallel, the fallback path is sequential.
findings.fix: In the exception handler, also use asyncio.gather for the fallback cache reads: fallback_tasks = [_get_cached_price(t) for t in tickers]; fallback_results = await asyncio.gather(*fallback_tasks).

findings.title: analytics/page.tsx section components load in parallel by default
findings.detail: The new component split causes MetricsSection, MonthlyReturnsSection, SectorFxSection, and HistorySection to all mount simultaneously, triggering 6+ API calls in parallel on first load. This is correct behavior and actually improves perceived performance by loading independently.
findings.fix: No fix needed — this is the desired behavior.
